description.value:
You are a descendant of the Alagondar family, the former rulers of Neverwinter. Lord Dagult Neverember, who claims the throne of Neverwinter, knows of your existence and sees you as a threat to his rule.

DM Note: At some point, Neverember may send an assassin — a nameless, middle-aged woman with a shaved head and a hook for a right hand — to kill this character.

You are a doppelganger who has assumed a humanoid identity. You can imitate specific NPCs and even other player characters.

DM Note: If this character's secret becomes widely known, the people of Ten-Towns will almost certainly try to kill or drive away the character out of fear.

You are an admirer of the legendary drow hero Drizzt Do'Urden, who is known throughout Icewind Dale. You came here partly in hopes of meeting him or walking in his footsteps.

DM Note: This harmless secret comes with no strings attached.

You are being pursued by a disgruntled pasha whose heart you broke. The pasha has hired an assassin to track you down, which is part of why you ended up in Icewind Dale.

DM Note: At a time of your choosing, an invisible stalker may show up to menace this character.

You were held captive by mind flayers aboard a nautiloid ship that crashed in Icewind Dale. The trauma of your captivity prevents you from remembering events prior to the crash.

DM Note: This character knows the location of the crashed nautiloid in chapter 2. A greater restoration spell removes the trauma and restores the character's memories of the other prisoners (a bulette and two carrion crawlers).

You were raised by yetis as a young child and still feel a kinship with them. Yetis do not attack you on sight and may even be friendly toward you.

DM Note: Yeti tykes are friendly toward this character by default.

You were born on the holiday of Midwinter, and Auril the Frostmaiden blessed you at birth. You are immune to the effects of extreme cold.

DM Note: If the party kills Auril, this character loses the benefits of this secret. The player might decide their character feels sympathy toward Auril because of the gift she bestowed.

One of the NPCs in Icewind Dale is your former romantic partner. The player chooses which NPC this is. This secret can turn an NPC foe into a potential ally.

DM Note: Let the player choose their character's secret lover. The lover can change each time you run this adventure with a different group.

You possess a runestone that allows you to summon the spirit of a fierce orc war chief named Vokarr the Eyebiter, who was killed by dwarves. The spirit looks like a living, breathing orc and speaks Common and Orc.

You have a special bond with owlbears and can change an owlbear's attitude to friendly with time and patience.

DM Note: If the character uses this secret to befriend an owlbear, it can become the character's companion and protector, run as an NPC.
You survived a shipwreck by doing things you are not proud of. You know the location of the Dark Duchess in chapter 2 and would be familiar with the ship's layout.

You are the secret offspring of Queen Bjornhild Solvigsdottir of the Reghed Tribe of the Tiger, discarded at birth. Bjornhild would not recognize you — and if you present yourself as her heir, she will attack you on sight, seeing you only as a rival and a sacrifice to Auril.

You died and were reincarnated in your current form. You retain fragments of memory from your past life, though the details are hazy. Roll on the How I Died table to determine your cause of death in your previous life.

DM Note: Work with the player to flesh out the details of the character's prior life and how it might tie into the adventure.

You are searching for a lost signet ring of great personal significance. It may be lodged inside Ol' Bitey, the stuffed knucklehead trout above the hearth at the Northlook in Bryn Shander. Each time a party member cuts open a trout pulled from Maer Dualdon, there is a 1% chance the fish has the ring inside it.

You wrote a book that angered the worshipers of Asmodeus, and now they want you dead. Three bearded devils (disguised in cold weather clothing) have been sent to kill you.

DM Note: The Knights of the Black Sword in Caer-Dineval, as followers of Levistus rather than Asmodeus, might warn this character that an attack is imminent and offer sanctuary.

A red slaad implanted an egg in you, and a slaad tadpole is growing inside your body. It will eventually burst from your chest if not removed. Any magic that cures a disease can end this threat before the tadpole emerges.

DM Note: The timing of the tadpole's birth is deliberately vague. There are moments in the adventure where the text suggests the emergence might occur.

You are a Harper spy assigned to Icewind Dale. You operate largely on your own, with little support from the organization. Your contact in Bryn Shander is Beldora, a chaotic good human spy who can share rumors and provide helpful advice.
